name: Cheese Pasta Roll with Tomato Sauce
recipeInstructions:
Bouquet garni is a cheesecloth bag containing rosemary, thyme, bay leaf, and crushed peppercorns. No specific quantity is required.
1. Prepare the tomatoes by briefly boiling them, then plunging into cold water and peeling off the skin. Remove seeds and chop.
2. Melt butter in a sauté pan and add minced shallots. Sauté for 5 minutes over low heat.
3. Add chopped tomatoes and bouquet garni to the pan. Season with salt and pepper. Cook covered over medium heat for 30 minutes.
4. Remove shallots and bouquet garni, then puree the sauce. Set aside.
Pasta Roll:
1. In a sauté pan, melt butter and add diced ham and scallions. Cook over medium heat until scallions are soft.
2. Beat cream cheese, Parmesan cheese, Ricotta cheese, and black pepper in a bowl until smooth.
3. Add the sauteed ham and scallions to the cheese mixture, then eggs. Mix well. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2-3 hours.
4. Spread half of the filling on one pasta sheet, leaving a half-inch border around the edges. Brush the border with melted butter.
5. Place sliced Prosciutto over the filling, then roll up starting from one short end. Roll tightly to prevent liquid from seeping in.
6. Wrap each roll in double-thick cheesecloth and tie securely at both ends.
7. Repeat for the second pasta sheet.
8. Fill a large sauté pan with chicken stock. Bring to a boil, then gently simmer the rolls, turning occasionally, for 25 minutes.
9. Remove from stock, unwrap, and let cool before cutting into thin slices.
10. Pour some tomato sauce into a pool at the bottom of serving dishes and arrange the slices on top.
11. Garnish with chopped chives. Serve warm or at room temperature.
